content.text:
```mdl
@ Non‑Disclosure Agreement – reverse‑engineering prohibition
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_7_nda_11

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
type Party = {
    name: string
}

type ConfidentialObject = {
    owner: Party,                     # party that owns the object (the Disclosing Party)
    embodies_confidential_info: bool # true iff the object contains Confidential Information
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entities – the stable public objects that can be aligned across modules
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
entity receiving_party: Party      # the “Recipient” of the Confidential Information
entity disclosing_party: Party     # the “Discloser” of the Confidential Information
entity confidential_object: ConfidentialObject

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper function – models the act of reverse‑engineering
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
func reverse_engineer(actor: Party, obj: ConfidentialObject) -> bool:
    # In a concrete deployment this predicate would be linked to an external
    # audit or monitoring system.  For the purpose of the logical model it is
    # a free Boolean that the solver may assign.
    false

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Obligation (O) – the Receiving Party must never reverse‑engineer any
# object that embodies the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 2.1 (handling of Confidential Information)
rule O not_reverse_engineer:
    let is_confidential =
        confidential_object.embodies_confidential_info and
        confidential_object.owner = disclosing_party
    (is_confidential implies not reverse_engineer(receiving_party, confidential_object)) always
```
